title: react.pdf
summaries:
* React components are composable, meaning a component can include one or more other components in its output.
* React apps are built by writing components that correspond to interface elements.
* These components are organized inside higher-level components that define the application's structure.
* Each component in a React app abides by strict data management principles.
* React's limited surface area provides tools to handle complex data and application state.

* React is a JavaScript framework.
* The surface area of React is limited.
* React aims to provide tools to anticipate how an application will look with a given set of circumstances.
* Using the framework involves including a JavaScript file in the HTML and using React exports in the application's JavaScript.
* The course digs into these principles later.

* The HTML document includes scripts for React (version 15.3.1), ReactDOM (version 15.3.1), and Babel (version 6). * A single line of JavaScript code dynamically adds "Hello world" to the page. * The script uses `ReactDOM.render` to insert the `

Hello world

` element into the HTML element with the ID `app`. * Only a handful of JavaScript files are required to get everything working. * React operates on a virtual DOM rather than directly on the browser's Document Object Model (DOM) immediately.

* React operates on a virtual DOM rather than directly on the browser's Document Object Model (DOM).
* Manipulating the browser's DOM after data changes can be slow.
* React resolves changes on a DOM built and run entirely in memory.
* React determines what changes to make to the actual browser's DOM after the virtual DOM is updated.

* The React Virtual DOM (https://facebook.github.io/react/docs/dom-differences.html) exists entirely in-memory and is a representation of the web browser's DOM.
* React components are written as virtual components that React turns into the DOM, rather than writing directly to the DOM.
* The next article covers building React components, JSX, and writing first real components.
* 4

* The React ecosystem includes ES6 and JSX.
* ES stands for ECMAScript.
* ES5 is the 5th update to JavaScript, finalized in 2009, and is basically "regular JavaScript."
* ES5 has been supported by all major browsers for several years.
* ES6 is a new version of JavaScript that adds new syntax.

* ES5 has been supported by all major browsers for several years.
* JavaScript written or seen in the recent past is likely ES5.
* ES6 is a version of JavaScript that adds syntactical and functional additions.
* ES6 was finalized in 2015.
* ES6 is almost fully supported by all major browsers (http://kangax.github.io/compat-table/es6/).
